title: Title
text:
GitHub Website

This is a sample abstract for the Discrete Random Structures II Conference. Please explain your subject briefly and include selected results.
Theorem. A major result.
Corollary. A noteworthy consequence.
Here is an equation: \[\tag{1}
a^2 + b^2 = c^2.\]
Refer to equations using math environment like this: \((1)\).



image caption


List references manually using numbers in square brackets in math environment: \([1]\), \([2]\), etc.
Bibliography
\([1]\) Ken-Iti, S. (1999). Lévy Processes and Infinitely Divisible Distributions. Cambridge University Press.
\([2]\) Jane Smith. "Title of the Article." Journal Name, vol. X, no. Y, Year, pp. Z. doi
\([3]\) Wikipedia article about Wrocław
\([4]\) Website of the Faculty of Pure and Applied Mathematics, Wrocław University of Science and Technology
\([5]\) Website of the Mathematical Institute, University of Wrocław

title: Abstract example
text:
GitHub Website

This is a sample abstract to showcase the flexibility of the HTML format. We still support LaTeX commands \[\begin{equation*}
    \int_\mathbb{R} f(x) \mathrm{d} x = \lim_{n \to \infty} \sum_{k} f(x_k) \Delta x_k.
\end{equation*}\]
Theorem. Theorems are stated as such, for example \[\begin{equation*}
    1+1=2
\end{equation*}\]
We support images



Figure caption


We also support multimedia embedding


We also support interactive elements (e.g. JavaScript). Here is a random walk on a random graph





List references manually using numbers in square brackets in math environment: \([1]\), \([2]\), etc.
Bibliography
\([1]\) Ken-Iti, S. (1999). Lévy Processes and Infinitely Divisible Distributions. Cambridge University Press.
\([2]\) Jane Smith. "Title of the Article." Journal Name, vol. X, no. Y, Year, pp. Z.
\([3]\) Wikipedia article about Wrocław
\([4]\) Website of the Faculty of Pure and Applied Mathematics, Wrocław University of Science and Technology
\([5]\) Website of the Mathematical Institute, University of Wrocław
